title: Recurring scheduler: cadence due-dates + idempotent `bober research tick`
description: Add deterministic cadence evaluation (compute nextDueAt from a job's cadence using an injected clock) and an idempotent `bober research tick` that runs only jobs whose nextDueAt is at or before the current time, advances each run job's nextDueAt and records lastRunAt, plus an optional `--watch` in-process loop. Document the scheduling-mechanism tradeoff.

successCriteria.description: computeNextDue(cadence, fromInstant) returns a deterministic next-due ISO timestamp for each supported cadence (e.g. daily/weekly/monthly) given an injected fromInstant, with no wall-clock read.
`bober research tick` with an injected now selects only jobs whose nextDueAt <= now (or have no nextDueAt yet) and skips jobs whose nextDueAt is after now.
After a job runs via tick, its stored nextDueAt advances by its cadence and lastRunAt equals the injected now; a second tick at the same now selects and runs zero jobs.
The project compiles with the scheduler module, the tick subcommand registered, and the optional `--watch` flag parsed.

nonGoals: Do not read the system clock inside cadence or scheduler logic — time is injected (Date.now only at the CLI boundary).
Do not change how a single run executes (Sprint 2 runner is invoked unchanged).
Do not add new egress or retrieval behavior (Sprint 3 owns that).
Do not depend on a hosted scheduler service; OS cron/launchd remains an external trigger documented in notes.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert deterministic computeNextDue, due-only selection, nextDueAt advancement after a run, and a no-op second tick at the same instant.
`bober research tick` runs due jobs through the Sprint 2 runner and persists updated lastRunAt/nextDueAt to the job JSON files.
definitionOfDone: A user (or an external OS cron entry) can run `bober research tick` to execute every job that is due as of now; cadence math is deterministic with an injected clock; each executed job's nextDueAt advances and lastRunAt is recorded, so re-running tick immediately runs nothing. An optional `--watch` loop is parsed. The tradeoff between in-repo loop, OS cron, and harness scheduler is documented in the CLI help/notes. Build is green.
assumptions: Canonical entrypoint is an idempotent `bober research tick`; OS cron/launchd is the recommended unattended trigger; `--watch` is an optional in-process loop (resolved Q1). Evidence: research doc L99-L100, L135, L141.
Cadence is the enum/string chosen in Sprint 1; computeNextDue maps it to a next instant from an injected base time (clock-free core, mirrors src/state/facts.ts).
tick updates job JSON files in place via the Sprint 1 job store; concurrency is single-process (no lock needed for the 2-person personal use case).
outOfScope: Retry/backoff policy for failed runs beyond logging the failure and leaving nextDueAt advanced.
generatorNotes: Create src/research/cadence.ts with computeNextDue(cadence, fromIso): string — pure, no Date.now (mirror the clock-free discipline in src/state/facts.ts). Create src/research/scheduler.ts with tick(deps) where deps = {now, runJob, jobStore}: load all jobs, filter nextDueAt <= now (or unset), invoke runJob (the Sprint 2 runResearchJob) for each, then set job.lastRunAt = now and job.nextDueAt = computeNextDue(job.cadence, now) and persist via the store. Add a `tick` subcommand to src/cli/commands/research.ts that injects Date.now at the boundary; add `--watch` to run tick on an interval (setInterval) but keep the loop body delegating to the same injected-now tick for testability. In the tick command help text, document the mechanism tradeoff: in-repo `--watch` loop (simple, dies with the process) vs OS cron/launchd calling `bober research tick` (survives reboots, recommended for unattended) vs a harness scheduler; note hosted-OAuth schedulers are unfit for unattended runs (research doc L135, L141).
evaluatorNotes: Assert computeNextDue is deterministic for fixed inputs across the supported cadences (sc-4-1). Build a temp job store with one due and one future job; run tick with a fixed now and assert only the due job's runJob spy fired (sc-4-2). Assert the executed job's persisted nextDueAt advanced by its cadence and lastRunAt === now, and a second tick at the same now fires zero runJob calls (sc-4-3). Confirm no Date.now/new Date() appears inside cadence.ts or scheduler.ts core (only at the CLI boundary). Confirm npm run build passes.
